title: Sushi Chef
description:
As a sushi chef in a Japanese restaurant, your responsibilities include handling a wide range of raw fish and other ingredients, managing food stock and working with an attention to detail to fulfil all orders within quality standards.

Responsibilities

  • Expertly cut, slice, and fillet different types of fish to prepare sushi dishes that meet quality and presentation standards
  • Inspect the quality of fruits, vegetables, and fish to ensure freshness and suitability for sushi preparation
  • Prepare various sushi dishes following established guidelines on portion size, presentation, and food safety
  • Maintain a clean and sanitary work environment to prevent food contamination and ensure compliance with hygiene standards
  • Regularly take inventory of food supplies and ingredients to manage stock levels and reduce waste
  • Report any issues with kitchen equipment promptly to the executive chef to minimize disruptions
  • Sterilize all utensils, instruments, and equipment before each use to maintain food safety standards
  • Communicate clearly with wait staff to accommodate special requests and food allergy considerations for customers
  • Engage with customers effectively to provide information and enhance their dining experience
  • Create new sushi items using daily fresh ingredients to contribute to menu variety and innovation

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Strong interpersonal skills to collaborate effectively with team members and provide excellent customer service
  • Ability to work well in a team environment and support colleagues in a fast-paced kitchen
  • Proficient bilingual communication skills in English and Mandarin, both written and spoken
  • Knowledge of Japanese language is an advantage but not essential
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in a similar sushi chef role

postedCompany.description:
From a single establishment, Les Amis Group has gone from strength to strength, capitalising on its commitment to quality in food, wine and service, to venture into other cuisine styles and concept. Today, Les Amis Group has 25 concepts, 1 virtual concepts and 38 restaurants in Singapore, and multiple overseas concepts including joint ventures and franchises in Hong Kong, Myanmar, Vietnam and Indonesia, with more expansion plans in the pipeline.

With award-winning concepts ranging from high-end French and Japanese restaurants to midmarket concepts offering Italian pizzas, Vietnamese noodles and authentic French bistro dining. Les Amis Group is undisputedly one of Singapore’s most influential and well-respected restaurant groups.

Les Amis is a group built on values of excellence, humility, inclusiveness, integrity and teamwork. Whether they are in fine-dining, Asian cuisine or casual dining, all our employees uphold the same values and fulfil a one-company approach to all that we do, from the relationships we build with our business partners and to the experience our guests and customers have at every touch-point across the Les Amis Group.
postedCompany.name: LES AMIS HOLDINGS PTE. LTD.
